keyPoints: The 'su' command allows a user to switch to another user account
Using 'su' without a username defaults to switching to the root user
The '-' option (su -) provides a login shell with the target user's environment
commonMistakes: Forgetting the '-' option and not getting the target user's full environment
Confusing 'su' (switch user) with 'sudo' (execute as another user)
Using 'su' without proper authorization or understanding the security implications
